2- Reactive scaling.
3- Since data are saved in a denormalized format, in Get ops we dont need to make lots of HTTP request to other services.
4- High Availability, if Primary server goes down, it swaps with secondary one.

Disadvantages:
1- Not strong in supporting ACID
2- No stored-procedure-like so you cant implement any business logic on db level.
3-

messages.text:
Hey there, curious minds! Let's dive into the exciting world of computer science and discover a fascinating concept called algorithms. Imagine you're in your kitchen, following a recipe to make your favorite chocolate chip cookies. The recipe guides you through the steps – measuring flour, cracking eggs, and mixing everything together – to create a delicious treat. In the same way, computers have their own set of instructions called algorithms. These algorithms are like digital recipes that guide computers on how to solve different problems step by step. Just like you follow a recipe to make cookies, computers follow algorithms to perform tasks.

Now, why are algorithms so important? Think about building a towering stack of colorful blocks. Each block needs to be carefully placed to create a stable structure. Similarly, algorithms organize the information and actions that computers need to handle, making sure everything works smoothly. They help computers make decisions, sort things, find patterns, and perform all sorts of tasks faster and more efficiently. Without algorithms, computers wouldn't know what to do or how to do it!

Imagine you're playing a game where you have to guess a secret number. Instead of guessing randomly, you can use an algorithm called "binary search." It's like a smart guessing game that saves you a ton of time. Imagine you have a list of numbers in order, and you need to find the right one. Binary search starts by looking at the middle number – if your guess is too high, you'll know to focus on the lower half; if it's too low, you'll focus on the upper half. With each step, you cut down your options in half, helping you find the secret number much faster than trying one number at a time.

Algorithms are like the superheroes of the digital world, helping computers make sense of things, solve problems, and be more efficient. Whether it's finding the fastest route on a map, suggesting your favorite songs on a music app, or even helping scientists analyze data, algorithms are everywhere in our tech-filled lives. So, keep your curiosity alive, and who knows, maybe one day you'll come up with an algorithm that changes the world!
